title: United Parks & Resorts (SeaWorld) Stock Faces Leisure Sector Headwinds Amid Cyclical Pressures
summary: United Parks & Resorts (SeaWorld) is experiencing cyclical pressures in the leisure sector due to economic uncertainties, causing its stock to be overshadowed by cruise lines. The company, operating theme parks like SeaWorld and Busch Gardens, relies on gate-driven revenue sensitive to consumer spending and economic slowdowns. Despite promotional efforts and a focus on marine life and conservation, PRKS's performance is highly correlated with discretionary spending, making it a higher-beta play for investors compared to more stable sectors.

title: Expedia Group Inc Stock (ISIN: US3024913036) Faces Headwinds Amid Event-Driven Optimism
summary: Expedia Group Inc (EXPE) is trading around $228, down significantly year-to-date despite strong long-term gains. The company is navigating a mixed market with near-term pressures but strategic upsides, including a new PredictHQ partnership to boost event-driven travel and benefiting from AI tailwinds. Analysts have a mixed outlook, with a consensus price target implying nearly 20% upside potential.

title: Booking Holdings Genius Program: Loyalty Revolution in Online Travel
summary: Booking Holdings' Genius program is a crucial loyalty initiative for Booking.com, significantly driving customer retention and revenue growth in the competitive online travel market. The program, which offers tiered perks, has seen increased adoption and contributes over 30% of total bookings on the platform, attracting higher-spending travelers. Investors see the Genius program as a key indicator of sustainable growth and market share expansion for Booking Holdings, with plans for further expansion into flights and car rentals.

title: Airbnb (ABNB) sells $2.5B in 2029, 2031 and 2036 senior notes
summary: Airbnb, Inc. has priced a multi-tranche offering of $2.5 billion in unsecured senior notes with maturities in 2029, 2031, and 2036. The net proceeds, estimated at $2.48 billion, are intended for general corporate purposes, including the repayment of $2.0 billion in outstanding 2026 notes. The notes are unsecured obligations ranking equally with existing senior debt, but are structurally subordinated to the liabilities of Airbnb's subsidiaries.

title: Mizuho Cites Booking (BKNG) As Top Internet Pick
summary: Mizuho has named Booking Holdings Inc. (BKNG) its top pick in the Internet sector, replacing Airbnb. This decision follows news that OpenAI is shifting away from direct checkout capabilities in ChatGPT towards app-based purchases, easing concerns that AI tools would circumvent online travel intermediaries. Booking Holdings Inc. saw its shares rise 8% on the news, reflecting renewed investor confidence in established travel platforms.

title: Blecharczyk reports multiple ABNB share sales (NASDAQ: ABNB)
summary: Nathan Blecharczyk, co-founder of Airbnb (NASDAQ: ABNB), and affiliated trusts reported multiple sales of ABNB Class A shares between December 19, 2025, and February 27, 2026. These routine insider dispositions include various transactions, such as the sale of 7,692 shares for over $1 million by the Nathan Blecharczyk 2020 Remainder Trust. The SEC Form 144 filing provides details on the number of shares sold and gross proceeds for each transaction, but does not include information on trading plans or tax-withholding.

title: Klarna Board Chair Michael Moritz Acquires 3.47 Million Shares for $50 Million
summary: Klarna Board Chair Michael Moritz purchased 3.47 million ordinary shares for nearly $50 million between March 3 and March 11, 2026. Additionally, Chief Product & Design Officer David Fock bought 27,000 shares for over $388,000. Meanwhile, Chief Marketing Officer David Sandström and Chief Commercial Officer David Sykes sold shares pursuant to established Rule 10b5-1 trading plans.

title: ABNB SEC Filings - Airbnb, Inc. 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K Forms
summary: This page provides a comprehensive resource for Airbnb (ABNB) SEC filings, including 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, and insider trading forms. It details how Airbnb uses these filings to communicate financial results, non-GAAP information, and changes in leadership. Investors can access a range of documents and utilize Stock Titan's tools to surface key information from these regulatory reports.

title: Adobe, Block, Ulta, Rubrik And Airbnb: Why These 5 Stocks Are On Investors' Radars Today
summary: Major U.S. indices closed lower on Thursday, impacting several prominent stocks. Adobe, Block, Ulta, Rubrik, and Airbnb were among the companies that garnered significant investor attention, with their stock performances influenced by earnings reports, sector-wide sell-offs, and macroeconomic factors like inflation and geopolitical tensions.

title: Booking Holdings Q4 2025 Financial Results and 25-for-1 Stock Split
summary: Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG) announced impressive Q4 2025 financial results, surpassing Wall Street expectations with record revenue and adjusted EPS. The company also declared a 25-for-1 stock split and a 9.4% dividend increase, signaling strong confidence and aiming to make shares more accessible to retail investors. These strategic moves, coupled with its "Connected Trip" vision, position Booking Holdings to navigate evolving market dynamics and maintain its leadership in the global travel industry despite broader economic concerns and competition.

title: Airbnb (NASDAQ:ABNB) Combines Strong Fundamentals with Bullish Technical Setup
summary: This article analyzes Airbnb (NASDAQ:ABNB) by combining fundamental and technical analysis, highlighting its strong market standing, profitability, and sound financial health, alongside a bullish technical setup. Airbnb scores highly in profitability and financial soundness, with impressive ROIC and profit margins, and is debt-free. Technically, the stock is showing positive short-term direction, consolidating before a potential breakout, making it an attractive candidate for investors seeking growth with controlled risk.

title: 1 Unpopular Stock That Deserves a Second Chance and 2 That Underwhelm
summary: This article identifies one stock, Airbnb (ABNB), as a potential buying opportunity despite Wall Street's pessimism, citing its consistent growth, strong EBITDA, and free cash flow margins. Conversely, it discourages investment in CarMax (KMX) due to poor sales, low margins, and high competition, and Great Lakes Dredge & Dock (GLDD) due to weak backlog growth, high production costs, and a history of cash burning. The analysis emphasizes unbiased research to find attractive investment candidates.

title: Airbnb (ABNB) director Gebbia’s Sycamore Trust sells 58K shares under plan
summary: Airbnb director and ten-percent owner Joseph Gebbia, through Sycamore Trust, sold 58,000 Class A Common Stock shares on March 9, 2026, under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. The shares were sold in open-market transactions with weighted average prices between $128.44 and $134.23. Following these sales, Sycamore Trust holds 286,015 Class A shares, and Gebbia directly holds 2,860 Class A shares.

title: A Look At Hilton Worldwide Holdings (HLT) Valuation After Recent Share Price Pullback
summary: Hilton Worldwide Holdings (HLT) has experienced a recent stock pullback despite strong long-term performance. While a narrative model suggests the stock is 9.1% undervalued with a fair value of $328.28, its current P/E ratio of 47x is significantly higher than industry averages, raising questions about potential overvaluation based on earnings. Investors are presented with conflicting signals regarding future potential.

title: Hilton Introduces the Hilton AI Planner, Advancing the Future of Curated Travel Discovery – Company Announcement
summary: Hilton has launched the Hilton AI Planner, a new generative AI-powered digital concierge available on hilton.com. This tool uses conversational intelligence to provide real-time responses and curated recommendations, aiming to make travel planning more intuitive and streamlined for guests. The Hilton AI Planner is currently in beta testing and signifies Hilton's ongoing commitment to innovation and enhancing the guest experience.

title: Booking Holdings (BKNG) Is Up 8.9% After Stock Split, Dividend Hike and AI Efficiency Gains – What's Changed
summary: Booking Holdings (BKNG) recently announced stronger-than-expected Q4 2025 results, a 25-for-1 stock split, a dividend increase, and positive 2026 guidance. The company also highlighted significant efficiency gains from its advanced AI tools, which reduced average cost per booking by about 10% in 2025. These developments strengthen Booking's investment narrative, particularly concerning profitability and margin expansion, while easing earlier market concerns about AI's potential to disrupt online travel agencies.

title: Expedia Stock Jumps 13% as AI Fears Ease. Analysts Still See Upside to $281
summary: Expedia Group's stock (EXPE) surged 13.7% after investors reevaluated risks posed by AI to online travel platforms, specifically after reports suggested OpenAI might scale back direct travel checkout plans within ChatGPT. The rally was also fueled by stronger-than-expected Q4 2025 earnings, with Expedia reporting adjusted earnings of $3.78 per share against estimates of $3.36, and analysts project further upside to $281. The company demonstrates strong financials, including high operating and gross margins due to its commission-based model, significant operating and free cash flow, and a conservative balance sheet, despite trading at lower valuation multiples than some competitors.

title: 6Z1.DE Earnings History & Surprises | EPS & Revenue Results | AIRBNB INC-CLASS A (FRA:6Z1)
summary: This article provides an overview of AIRBNB INC-CLASS A (FRA:6Z1) earnings history, including past quarterly EPS and revenue results, analyst estimates, and surprise percentages. It details the upcoming earnings release, the last reported figures, and historical performance data spanning several quarters. Key analysis points include the company's beat rates for EPS and revenue over the last four quarters.

title: Is Marriott Vacations Worldwide (VAC) Fairly Priced After Recent Share Price Rebound
summary: Marriott Vacations Worldwide (VAC) has seen mixed share price performance recently, with significant short-term gains but longer-term declines. Valuation analysis using a Discounted Cash Flow model suggests the stock is slightly undervalued by 2.9% at $72.57 per share compared to its current price of $70.50, indicating it is "about right." Additionally, its Price/Sales ratio of 0.73x is below both industry averages and Simply Wall St's "Fair Ratio" of 2.19x, leading to an "undervalued" assessment by this metric.

title: Booking Holdings’ AI Push Tests Role In Trip Planning And Payments
summary: Booking Holdings is integrating advanced AI tools into its brands like Booking.com and Priceline to adapt to evolving travel search behaviors. The company aims to leverage its "merchant of record" role to maintain its central position in trip planning and payments, even as large AI platforms influence initial discovery. This strategic move, however, introduces execution risks regarding conversion and potential margin compression, alongside competitive pressures from other online travel agencies.

title: Booking Holdings Stock Surges As AI-Checkout Fears Ease Thursday
summary: Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG) shares surged after reports indicated OpenAI is scaling back native ChatGPT checkout, easing fears of AI-driven disruption in online travel. Mizuho upgraded Booking to its Top Pick, suggesting a potential rebound for Online Travel Agencies (OTAs) that were negatively impacted by AI-checkout concerns. Analysts largely remain constructive on BKNG, despite some target adjustments, as the stock bounced off recent lows.

title: Uber Shares Sold by Investment Firm
summary: JT Stratford LLC, an institutional investor, has significantly reduced its stake in Uber Technologies, Inc. by selling 33,521 shares in the third quarter of 2026, lowering its holdings to 7,242 shares. This 82.2% reduction, valuing their remaining stake at $709,000, signals investor caution regarding Uber's stock performance amidst ongoing challenges like litigation and regulatory issues. The move highlights continued uncertainty about Uber's long-term prospects among major institutional investors.

title: Travel Accommodation Market Set to Witness Rapid Growth Through
summary: Coherent Market Insights has released a comprehensive report on the Travel Accommodation Market, forecasting rapid growth through 2033. The study delves into industry trends, competitive dynamics, regional performance, and growth potential, providing essential insights for businesses. Key players like Marriott International Inc., Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc., and Airbnb Inc. are highlighted, with segmentation by type and application.

title: Trip.com Group (NasdaqGS:TCOM) Valuation After Antitrust Probe And Strong 2025 Earnings
summary: Trip.com Group (TCOM) is facing an antitrust investigation and shareholder litigation, causing its share price to decline despite strong 2025 earnings. Analysts suggest the stock is significantly undervalued at $50.86 compared to a fair value of $87.08, driven by investments in AI and integrated trip planning tools. However, the antitrust probe and increasing direct bookings pose risks to this valuation narrative.

title: Booking Holdings Stock Surges As AI-Checkout Fears Ease Thursday
summary: Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG) shares surged on Thursday after analyst commentary linked the stock's rise to easing fears about AI-driven disruption in online travel. This shift comes as OpenAI reportedly reduces its efforts in ChatGPT checkout, leading analysts to believe online travel agencies like Booking Holdings could rebound. Mizuho analyst Lloyd Walmsley highlighted Booking as a top pure-play OTA, suggesting a potential move towards traditional search and cost-per-click models for AI shopping, which would favor established marketplaces.

title: Tripadvisor reviews under the microscope: Can you still trust the stars?
summary: This article examines the reliability of Tripadvisor reviews in 2026, noting that while the platform remains a powerful planning tool, users need to understand its complexities and limitations. It delves into how ratings are built, filtered, and sometimes manipulated, advising travelers on how to spot trustworthy reviews amidst increasing commercial influence and fake content. The piece concludes that Tripadvisor reviews are valuable when used critically, not as a definitive guarantee, especially for US travelers and investors watching Tripadvisor Inc. (TRIP).

title: Booking Holdings Stock Surges As AI-Checkout Fears Ease Thursday
summary: Booking Holdings Inc. shares rose on Thursday after an analyst report suggested fears of AI-driven disruption in online travel may be easing. Mizuho upgraded Booking to its top pick following news that OpenAI is scaling back native ChatGPT checkout features, indicating a potential rebound for online travel agencies. This shift, combined with largely constructive Wall Street ratings, suggests the company could benefit from AI shopping evolving to a click-out model rather than direct on-platform purchases.

title: Booking Holdings (BKNG) stock jumps as OpenAI cools ChatGPT checkout plans
summary: Booking Holdings (BKNG) shares surged after a report indicated OpenAI is scaling back its plans for direct checkout within ChatGPT, alleviating concerns about AI disruption for online travel agencies. Mizuho upgraded Booking to its top pick over Airbnb, noting that OpenAI is now moving towards app-based transactions through partners like Booking.com. Despite the positive news, geopolitical conflicts in the Middle East causing flight disruptions and higher fuel prices remain a challenge for the travel industry.

title: Is Booking Stock Outperforming Its Rivals?
summary: Booking Holdings (BKNG) stock has underperformed in the past year despite strong profitability and revenue growth, raising questions about its standing against competitors in the digital travel sector. The article compares BKNG's metrics like operating margin, LTM revenue growth, and PE ratio with peers such as Expedia, Airbnb, Marriott, Hilton, and Tripadvisor, highlighting its operational efficiency and strategic shifts. While BKNG shows robust fundamentals, investor concerns over macro risks and AI disruption have led to its recent stock drop.

title: Airbnb (ABNB) CFO Elinor Mertz sells 3,750 shares under 10b5-1 plan
summary: Airbnb's CFO, Elinor Mertz, sold 3,750 shares of Class A common stock at $130.00 per share in an open-market transaction. This sale was conducted under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted on May 30, 2025. Following this transaction, Mertz directly holds 402,416.681 shares of the company.

title: Citi cuts IHG target price and urges investors to wait for better entry point
summary: Citi has reduced its target price for Intercontinental Hotels Group (IHG) to $115 and maintained a 'sell' recommendation. The bank forecasts slower adjusted earnings growth for IHG than the company's own mid-term target, citing weakening US lodging demand and increased competition from short-term rental platforms. Citi advises investors to await a more attractive entry point before purchasing IHG shares.

title: 110-night Grand Voyage: Celebrity Solstice gets bow-to-stern makeover
summary: Celebrity Cruises has relaunched its Celebrity Solstice ship after a bow-to-stern makeover, introducing eight new experiences, including four new-to-brand spaces and 54 additional staterooms. The revitalized ship departed Singapore on March 2, 2026, and will embark on a 110-night Grand Voyage on September 13, 2026, exploring Asia, Australia, New Zealand, the South Pacific, and Alaska. Enhancements include new dining venues, entertainment spaces, a top-deck Sunset Park, and upgraded accommodations, aiming to elevate the guest experience.

title: Elinor Mertz sells ABNB Class A shares in multiple transactions (ABNB)
summary: Elinor Mertz, an insider at Airbnb (ABNB), has reported multiple proposed sales of Class A common stock through a Form 144 SEC filing. The transactions include disposing of 4,630 shares for $578,750 on December 9, 2025; 7,500 shares for $975,000 on December 12, 2025; 3,750 shares for $509,775 on January 2, 2026; and 3,750 shares for $487,500 on February 2, 2026. Fidelity Brokerage Services LLC is listed as the broker for these transactions.

title: Expedia Group (EXPE): From Tech Unification to B2B Powerhouse – 2026 Research Feature
summary: Expedia Group (EXPE) has transitioned from a fragmented "house of brands" to a unified tech platform, with its B2B segment emerging as a high-margin powerhouse. CEO Ariane Gorin's "B2B-first" strategy and the global rollout of the One Key loyalty program aim to bridge the valuation gap with competitors like Booking Holdings and Airbnb. Despite recent stock volatility and marketing spend challenges, the company's B2B growth and AI-driven innovations offer significant future catalysts.

title: RCL Financials: Income Statement, Balance Sheet & Cash Flow
summary: This article provides a detailed financial overview of Royal Caribbean Group (RCL) for fiscal year 2025, including income statements, balance sheets, cash flow statements, and key financial ratios. It highlights the company's strong profitability, significant revenue growth, and robust return on equity, while also noting areas like liquidity where improvements could be made. The analysis incorporates various financial health indicators such as Altman Z-Score and Piotroski F-Score to assess overall financial standing.

title: Expedia Group Inc.: Is This Travel Stock Still a Buy for 2026?
summary: This article analyzes Expedia Group Inc. as a travel stock, focusing on its recent pivot towards AI, loyalty programs, and simplified technology to capture a larger share of the US travel market. It examines how Expedia is leveraging its brands like Hotels.com and Vrbo to create a unified ecosystem, aiming to enhance the user experience and expand its market position against competitors like Booking and Airbnb. The piece also delves into the stock's sensitivity to US economic cycles and consumer spending, offering insights for both travelers and investors on its potential as a long-term buy heading into 2026.

title: Pursuit’s 2025 Success Powers 2026 Growth Strategy For Iconic Destinations
summary: Pursuit Attractions and Hospitality reported strong 2025 results with a 23.4% revenue increase, reaching $452.4 million, driven by strategic investments and demand for iconic destinations despite challenges like wildfires. The company anticipates continued growth in 2026 with a revenue guidance of $465 million and a 9% increase in Adjusted EBITDA, supported by new market expansions, acquisitions, and a focus on sustainable, experiential tourism. Its Vision 2030 plan aims to exceed $845 million in revenue through performance elevation, organic growth, acquisitions, and capital deployment, emphasizing guest experience and environmental responsibility.

title: COOLU - Corner Growth Acquisition Corp Latest Stock News & Market Updates
summary: This page provides dedicated news and market updates for Corner Growth Acquisition Corp (COOLU), compiling articles, expert summaries from Rhea-AI, and end-of-day stock performance. It aims to offer insights for investors on how various news events could impact the stock, categorized by tags for easy navigation. The content includes historical news events like business combinations, IPOs, and key appointments.

summary: This page provides comprehensive access to Airbnb (ABNB) SEC filings, including annual reports (10-K), quarterly reports (10-Q), material events (8-K), and insider trading forms (Form 4). title: Airbnb (ABNB) CAO disposes 804.937 shares to satisfy tax withholding
summary: Airbnb's Chief Accounting Officer, David C. Bernstein, disposed of 804.937 shares of Class A Common Stock on February 19, 2026, at a price of $124.27 per share. This transaction was a tax-withholding disposition to cover tax liabilities, not an open-market sale. Following this event, Bernstein directly holds 39,232.033 shares.

title: Payoneer Global (NASDAQ:PAYO) Releases Quarterly Earnings Results, Misses Expectations By $0.01 EPS
summary: Payoneer Global (NASDAQ:PAYO) reported quarterly earnings, missing analyst expectations by $0.01 EPS with revenue of $274.69 million against an estimated $282.79 million. Despite a strong 2025 performance, including 14% revenue growth ex-interest and robust Adjusted EBITDA, the company's shares fell 18.5% following the announcement and a cautious 2026 outlook. Analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average price target of $8.71.

title: New Marriott Vacations Leaders Step In As Stock Screens Undervalued
summary: Marriott Vacations Worldwide (NYSE:VAC) has appointed Matthew E. Avril as CEO and Michael A. Flaskey as President and COO, effective February 17, 2026. These leadership changes come as the stock screens as undervalued by Simply Wall St, trading 33.3% below estimated fair value despite recent positive momentum. Investors will be observing how the new leadership addresses the company's current financial challenges, including a loss of US$308m and concerns about interest coverage and dividend sustainability.

title: Airbnb, Inc. Shs Thailand Depositary Receipts Repr 1 Sh
summary: This page provides an overview of Airbnb, Inc. Shs Thailand Depositary Receipts Repr 1 Sh, including its trading information, research coverage statistics, and related securities. It highlights that an average of 16 research reports are published annually, with a total of 128 reports available on Research Tree from 3 providers. The security trades under the ticker ABNB06 TB on The Stock Exchange of Thailand.

title: Founder Nathan Blecharczyk lists multiple Class A sales (NASDAQ: ABNB)
summary: Nathan Blecharczyk, a founder of Airbnb (NASDAQ: ABNB), has reported numerous sales of Class A shares, as detailed in a recent Form 144 SEC filing. These transactions include dispositions by Blecharczyk himself and related trusts, listing individual sale dates, share counts, and dollar amounts for transparency. The filing indicates regular insider selling activity, with notable sales such as 141,063 shares on December 8, 2025, and 61,033 shares on December 4, 2025.

title: Expedia Hotel Booking Is Quietly Changing: Should You Trust It Now?
summary: Expedia has evolved its hotel booking experience with advanced AI search, unified One Key loyalty perks, and greater fee transparency for US travelers. While the platform offers faster hotel discovery, clearer pricing, and improved customer support, users should still cross-check prices with direct hotel bookings and be aware of potential resort fees. Expedia remains a strong choice for comparison shopping and package deals, but a hybrid approach combining Expedia's research tools with direct booking for specific benefits is recommended.

title: Marriott Vacations Worldwide (VAC) Quarterly EPS Loss Tests Bullish Earnings Recovery Narrative
summary: Marriott Vacations Worldwide (VAC) reported a significant EPS loss of US$12.42 in Q4 2025 and a net income loss of US$308 million for the trailing 12 months, despite stable revenue. This challenges the bullish narrative of strong growth and recovery. Concerns are also raised about dividend coverage and interest payments, which are not currently supported by earnings, leading to a wide valuation gap between its DCF fair value and market price.

title: Westpac Banking Corp Sells 8,681 Shares of Expedia Group, Inc. $EXPE
summary: Westpac Banking Corp has reduced its stake in Expedia Group by 44.8%, selling 8,681 shares and retaining 10,701 shares valued at $2.287 million. Other institutional investors like Bessemer Group Inc. and First Horizon Advisors Inc. increased their positions. Expedia Group reported strong quarterly earnings, beating expectations with EPS of $3.78 and revenue of $3.55 billion, and increased its quarterly dividend to $0.48 per share.

title: [Form 4] Airbnb, Inc. Insider Trading Activity
summary: Airbnb, Inc. director and 10% owner Joseph Gebbia, through the Sycamore Trust, sold 58,000 shares of Class A common stock on February 23, 2026. The sales were conducted under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan at weighted-average prices between $120.03 and $128.29. Following these transactions, Sycamore Trust holds 344,015 Class A shares, and Gebbia directly holds 2,860 shares.

title: Choice Hotels Announces New Cash Dividend, Payable On April 15, 2026, Highlighting its Impact on the Hospitality Sector
summary: Choice Hotels International has announced a cash dividend of $0.2875 per share, payable on April 15, 2026, to shareholders recorded on April 1, 2026. This move demonstrates the company's financial strength and commitment to returning value to investors, solidifying its position as a leader in the lodging franchising industry. The dividend declaration also signals resilience and stability for the broader hospitality sector, particularly in the franchised lodging market.
